choices.text: Ask where they were
Ask what they saw
Probe the timeline
Establish their own whereabouts
Ask what they knew about the people involved
Ask about any unusual behavior they noticed
Push them on the details
Ask what they think happened

nodes.text: I was home. Same as most evenings. I tend to sit by the window. You notice things from there.

choices.text: Which window? How close is it to {place}?
Were you alone?

nodes.text: Faces right out toward {place}. I have a clear line of sight from my chair. Better than most in this building, I can tell you that.
Just me and the cat. She does not make for much of a witness, but I do. I was there the whole evening.
I noticed something near {place} around {time_phrase}. A figure. Moving like they wanted to go unnoticed.

choices.text: Describe exactly what you saw.
How good was your view?

nodes.text: Not running, but purposeful. Head slightly down. You know the walk people use when they are trying to look casual and failing at it.
Good enough. There is a lamp just outside {place}. Decent light. I am not saying I could read a nameplate, but a face — yes.
Around {time_phrase}, I would say. Give or take. I have a sense of time even without checking.

choices.text: How are you placing that estimate?
What were you doing at that moment?

nodes.text: My program finishes at a fixed time. This was just after. So {time_phrase} is about right. Probably within fifteen minutes.
Making tea. That is my habit at that hour. I had just put the kettle on when I noticed the movement outside.
Home all evening. I can tell you the weather, the sounds, the cars that came and went. I pay attention.

choices.text: Can anyone confirm that?
Did you step out at any point?

nodes.text: The couple on the floor below. They heard me moving around — thin walls in this building. They can confirm I was in.
Once. To check the post. Down and back in five minutes, no more. I may have missed something in that window, but only five minutes.
I knew them well enough to nod. Saw them coming and going. They kept interesting hours, I will say that.

choices.text: What do you mean by interesting hours?
Did you ever see anyone visiting them?

nodes.text: Late nights. Early mornings. Visitors at odd times. I am not judging — people live how they live. But you notice patterns.
There was a man. Came around a few times in the last month. Loud voice. The kind of conversation you hear through a wall even when you are not trying to.
Something changed about the mood around {place} in the last few weeks. You can feel these things in a building.

choices.text: What specifically changed?
Did you notice anything about {suspect} in particular?

nodes.text: Curtains drawn more. Less coming and going. The kind of quiet that is not peaceful — it is cautious. Like someone waiting.
That one had been checking over their shoulder. Noticed it twice. People do that when they are watching for something they are not sure will come from.
I am telling you what I observed. People look at the same thing from different angles. That does not make me wrong.

choices.text: Some of your details do not line up.
You may have misread what you saw.

nodes.text: I know what I saw. I have been watching this block for years. I know what normal looks like around here and what does not fit.
I could be wrong on the exact time. I am not wrong about the figure or the direction they were heading. Those I am certain of.
What do I think happened? I have my ideas. People around here always have ideas.

choices.text: Do you think someone finally snapped?
Could this have been a situation that got out of hand?
Is it possible someone was protecting another person?
Could it have been unintentional?

nodes.text: That family had a way of making enemies without realizing it. Or maybe they did realize it. Either way, the friction was real. Everyone in the building knew.
Something was building for a while. You could feel it. Like pressure before a storm. One of those situations that only needs one small thing to go wrong.
There were people in that picture who needed looking after. Whether someone took things too far trying to do that — I could believe it.
I would not call it accidental from what I saw that evening. But I was at a window, not in the room. So what do I know.
